   "source": [
    "# Tutorial: Transfer Function Measurement\n",
    "\n",
    "[![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/tatsuki-washimi/gwexpy/blob/main/docs/web/en/user_guide/tutorials/case_transfer_function.ipynb)\n",
    "\n",
    "This tutorial demonstrates how to use `gwexpy` to estimate the transfer function of a system from measured data,\n",
    "fit a physical model to the transfer function, and identify system parameters.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Scenario:**\n",
    "We inject white noise into a mechanical vibration system (or electrical circuit) and measure its response.\n",
    "From this input-output data, we will determine the system's resonance frequency $f_0$ and quality factor $Q$.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Workflow:**\n",
    "1.  **Data generation**: Simulate an input signal (white noise) and the system output (resonant system response + measurement noise).\n",
    "2.  **Transfer function estimation**: Calculate the transfer function and coherence from the input-output time series data and plot the Bode plot.\n",
    "3.  **Model fitting**: Fit a theoretical model (such as a Lorentzian function) to the estimated transfer function to extract system parameters."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "f87a9082",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-09T19:29:35.775194Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-09T19:29:35.774944Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-09T19:29:48.549156Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-09T19:29:48.547665Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 12.785305,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:29:48.549889+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:29:35.764584+00:00",
     "status": "completed"

   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=UserWarning)\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=DeprecationWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "from scipy import signal\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy import TimeSeries\n",
    "from gwexpy.plot import Plot"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "ee8e43ca",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.011097,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:29:48.569335+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:29:48.558238+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 1. Generating Experimental Data (Simulation)\n",
    "\n",
    "We will conduct a virtual experiment.\n",
    "*   **System**: Second-order resonant system (harmonic oscillator)\n",
    "    *   Resonance frequency $f_0 = 300$ Hz\n",
    "    *   Quality factor $Q = 50$\n",
    "*   **Input**: White noise\n",
    "*   **Measurement**: Sampling frequency 2048 Hz, duration 60 seconds\n",
    "\n",
    "The output signal will also include measurement noise."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "bf76531d",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-09T19:29:48.619619Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-09T19:29:48.619226Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-09T19:30:40.385374Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-09T19:30:40.384827Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 51.810483,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:30:40.416760+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:29:48.606277+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Input data shape: (122880,)\n",
      "Output data shape: (122880,)\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 900x1000 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# --- Parameter Settings: choose a resonator whose f0 and Q mimic a narrow mechanical mode worth identifying from measured data. ---\n",
    "fs = 2048.0  # Sampling frequency [Hz]\n",
    "duration = 60.0  # Duration [s]\n",
    "f0_true = 300.0  # True resonance frequency [Hz]\n",
    "Q_true = 50.0  # True quality factor\n",
    "\n",
    "# --- Time axis and input signal: broadband drive excites the plant across frequency so the resonance can be measured in one experiment. ---\n",
    "t = np.linspace(0, duration, int(duration * fs), endpoint=False)\n",
    "input_data = np.random.normal(0, 1, size=len(t))  # Broadband excitation approximates a swept identification drive without privileging one frequency.\n",
    "\n",
    "# --- Physical System Simulation (using scipy.signal): this second-order plant stands in for a suspension or actuator resonance. ---\n",
    "# H(s) uses f0 for the modal frequency and Q for damping width, so fitting them back out tests whether the measurement preserves the true mode shape.\n",
    "w0 = 2 * np.pi * f0_true\n",
    "num = [w0**2]\n",
    "den = [1, w0 / Q_true, w0**2]\n",
    "system = signal.TransferFunction(num, den)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Simulate the time response to the broadband drive so transfer estimation sees the same finite-duration effects as a real measurement.\n",
    "_, output_clean, _ = signal.lsim(system, U=input_data, T=t)\n",
    "\n",
    "# --- Add measurement noise: coherence later tells us whether this readout noise is low enough to trust the estimated transfer function. ---\n",
    "# Treat this as sensor/readout noise, which degrades phase and gain estimates where the plant response is weak.\n",
    "measurement_noise = np.random.normal(0, 0.1, size=len(t))\n",
    "output_data = output_clean + measurement_noise\n",
    "\n",
    "# --- Wrap the drive and response as TimeSeries so the same workflow applies to experimental channels. ---\n",
    "ts_input = TimeSeries(input_data, t0=0, sample_rate=fs, name=\"Input\", unit=\"V\")\n",
    "ts_output = TimeSeries(output_data, t0=0, sample_rate=fs, name=\"Output\", unit=\"V\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Input data shape:\", ts_input.shape)\n",
    "print(\"Output data shape:\", ts_output.shape)\n",
    "Plot(ts_input, ts_output, separate=True);\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "097ada5e",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.024172,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:30:40.470827+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:30:40.446655+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 2. Transfer Function Estimation\n",
    "\n",
    "From the input-output time series data `ts_input` and `ts_output`, we estimate the frequency response function (transfer function).\n",
    "We use the `transfer_function` method of `gwexpy`.\n",
    "\n",
    "We also calculate the **coherence** to assess the reliability of the measurement.\n",
    "Frequency bands where the coherence is close to 1 indicate that the input-output relationship is linear and the influence of noise is small."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "9706b8ae",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-09T19:30:40.590493Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-09T19:30:40.590226Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.238757Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.238067Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 4.676577,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.239830+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:30:40.563253+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x1000 with 3 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# FFT settings: average 2-second segments so random measurement noise is reduced without washing out the 300 Hz resonance.\n",
    "fftlength = 2.0  # FFT every 2 seconds and average\n",
    "\n",
    "# Estimate Output/Input so the plant response appears directly in gain and phase.\n",
    "tf = ts_input.transfer_function(ts_output, fftlength=fftlength)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Coherence is the confidence check: low coherence means the response is noise dominated and the transfer estimate should not be over-interpreted.\n",
    "coh = ts_input.coherence(ts_output, fftlength=fftlength) ** 0.5\n",
    "\n",
    "# --- Plot Bode magnitude, phase, and coherence together so trustworthiness and fitted physics can be read on the same axis. ---\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(10, 10), sharex=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Magnitude\n",
    "ax = axes[0]\n",
    "ax.loglog(tf.abs(), label=\"Measured TF\")\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel(\"Gain [V/V]\")\n",
    "ax.set_title(\"Bode Plot: Magnitude\")\n",
    "ax.grid(True, which=\"both\", alpha=0.5)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Phase\n",
    "ax = axes[1]\n",
    "ax.plot(tf.degree(), label=\"Measured Phase\")\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel(\"Phase [deg]\")\n",
    "ax.set_title(\"Bode Plot: Phase\")\n",
    "ax.set_yticks(np.arange(-180, 181, 90))\n",
    "ax.grid(True, which=\"both\", alpha=0.5)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Coherence\n",
    "ax = axes[2]\n",
    "ax.plot(coh, color=\"green\", label=\"Coherence\")\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel(\"Coherence\")\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel(\"Frequency [Hz]\")\n",
    "ax.set_ylim(0, 1.1)\n",
    "ax.set_xlim(10, 1000)\n",
    "ax.grid(True, which=\"both\", alpha=0.5)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "978f6dbd",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.02376,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.289167+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.265407+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 3. Model Fitting\n",
    "\n",
    "We fit a theoretical model to the obtained transfer function (especially around the resonance).\n",
    "Here, we define a transfer function model for a harmonic oscillator and use least-squares fitting to determine the parameters ($A, f_0, Q$).\n",
    "\n",
    "Model equation (including gain $A$):\n",
    "$$ H(f) = A \\cdot \\frac{f_0^2}{f_0^2 - f^2 + i (f f_0 / Q)} $$"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "469b06e9",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.359253Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.358987Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.426612Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.425925Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.084584,
     "end_time": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.427595+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-06-09T19:30:45.343011+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n",
      "│                                Migrad                                   │\n",
      "├──────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┤\n",
      "│ FCN = 6.473 (χ²/ndof = 0.0)      │              Nfcn = 118              │\n",
      "│ EDM = 4.35e-06 (Goal: 0.0002)    │                                      │\n",
      "├──────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤\n",
      "│          Valid Minimum           │   Below EDM threshold (goal x 10)    │\n",
      "├──────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤\n",
      "│      No parameters at limit      │           Below call limit           │\n",
      "├──────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤\n",
      "│             Hesse ok             │         Covariance accurate          │\n",
      "└──────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┘\n",
      "┌───┬──────┬───────────┬───────────┬────────────┬────────────┬─────────┬─────────┬───────┐\n",
      "│   │ Name │   Value   │ Hesse Err │ Minos Err- │ Minos Err+ │ Limit-  │ Limit+  │ Fixed │\n",
      "├───┼──────┼───────────┼───────────┼────────────┼────────────┼─────────┼─────────┼───────┤\n",
      "│ 0 │ amp  │   0.935   │   0.007   │            │            │         │         │       │\n",
      "│ 1 │ f0   │  299.996  │   0.021   │            │            │         │         │       │\n",
      "│ 2 │ Q    │   49.4    │    0.5    │            │            │         │         │       │\n",
      "└───┴──────┴───────────┴───────────┴────────────┴────────────┴─────────┴─────────┴───────┘\n",
      "┌─────┬────────────────────────────┐\n",
      "│     │      amp       f0        Q │\n",
      "├─────┼────────────────────────────┤\n",
      "│ amp │ 4.34e-05       -0 -2.29e-3 │\n",
      "│  f0 │       -0 0.000455  -0.1e-3 │\n",
      "│   Q │ -2.29e-3  -0.1e-3     0.24 │\n",
      "└─────┴────────────────────────────┘\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# --- Define a resonator model whose parameters map back to measurable plant physics. ---\n",
    "def resonator_model(f, amp, f0, Q):\n",
    "    # f: frequency array in Hz where the measured plant response is sampled.\n",
    "    # amp scales the actuator/sensor gain, f0 is the modal resonance, and Q controls how sharply energy stays trapped near that mode.\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Use the complex response so the fit respects both magnitude and phase, not just the height of the peak.\n",
    "    numerator = amp * (f0**2)\n",
    "    denominator = (f0**2) - (f**2) + 1j * (f * f0 / Q)\n",
    "    return numerator / denominator\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "# --- Fit only the resonance-dominated band so broadband tails do not bias the modal parameters. ---\n",
    "# Fitting the full band would force one second-order model to explain off-resonance structure it was never meant to capture,\n",
    "# so we isolate 100-500 Hz where the identified mode dominates the response.\n",
    "tf_crop = tf.crop(100, 500)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Seed f0 near the visible peak so the optimizer lands on the physical resonance instead of a spurious local minimum.\n",
    "# The initial guess is intentionally close to the observed resonance around 300 Hz.\n",
    "p0 = {\"amp\": 1.0, \"f0\": 300.0, \"Q\": 10.0}\n",
    "\n",
    "# Fit the complex transfer function in this narrowed band.\n",
    "# .fit() uses both real and imaginary parts, which preserves phase information needed to recover damping consistently.\n",
    "result = tf_crop.fit(resonator_model, p0=p0)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(result)\n"
   ]
  },

    "## 4. Verification of Results\n",
    "\n",
    "We verify the fitting results numerically and plot them overlaid on the measured data.\n",
    "The `FitResult.plot()` method automatically generates a Bode plot (magnitude and phase) for complex data."
   ]
  },

    "# --- Report the recovered modal parameters so they can be compared with the known plant values. ---\n",
    "print(\"--- Estimated Parameters ---\")\n",
    "print(f\"Resonance Frequency (f0): {result.params['f0']:.4f} Hz (True: {f0_true})\")\n",
    "print(f\"Quality Factor (Q):       {result.params['Q']:.4f}      (True: {Q_true})\")\n",
    "print(f\"Gain (Amp):               {result.params['amp']:.4f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# --- Plot the fitted response to confirm that both the peak height and phase rotation are captured. ---\n",
    "# result.plot() returns magnitude and phase axes because both observables are needed to judge whether the modal model is credible.\n",
    "axes = result.plot()\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  }
